content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "C+=". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1368/A
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. The first line of each test case contains two integers n and k. The second line contains n integers a1, a2, ..., an.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of operations required to make all elements of the array greater than or equal to k.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the array and then find the minimum number of operations required to make all elements greater than or equal to k.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n, k = map(int, input().split())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    arr.sort()
    count = 0
    for i in range(n):
        if arr[i] < k:
            count += 1
    print(count)
